name: Exploit/Malware Events Across Multiple Destinations
notes: This rule triggers when exploit or malware events are seen on multiple destination hosts. This could indicate a malicious software or an attacker exploiting vulnerable hosts on the network.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same source IP more than 5 times
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following Destination Vulnerable to Detected Exploit on a Different Port
when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 5 destination IP within 5 minutes
thresholds: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 5 destination IP within 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Exploit/Malware Events Across Multiple Destinations on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following Destination Vulnerable to Detected Exploit on a Different Port
AND when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 5 destination IP within 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Exploit/Malware Events Across Multiple Targets
Event description: Detects when exploit or malware events are seen on multiple destination hosts. This could indicate a malicious software or an attacker exploiting vulnerable hosts on the network.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
